steps.title: Use the tangent ratio
steps.content: From the figure, $AC=90$ is the hypotenuse. For angle $C$, tangent compares the opposite side to the adjacent side, so $AB$ and $BC$ are in a $3$ to $4$ ratio.

steps.title: Set up the side lengths
steps.content: Write the two legs as $AB=3m$ and $BC=4m$. Those leg lengths form a $3$-$4$-$5$ right triangle, so the hypotenuse is $5m$.

steps.title: Find the scale factor
steps.content:
Use the hypotenuse to solve for the multiplier:
$$5m=90$$
$$m=18$$

steps.title: Find the requested side
steps.content: The question asks for $AB$, the side opposite angle $C$. Since $AB=3m$, substitute $m=18$: $$AB=3(18)=54$$ The answer is C.
